title: Augmentation Varieties and Disk Potential
speaker: Soham Chanda
abstract: Dimitroglou-Rizell-Golovko constructs a family of Legendrians in prequantization bundles by taking lifts of monotone Lagrangians. These lifted Legendrians have a Morse-Bott family of Reeb chords. We construct a version of Legendrian Contact Homology(LCH) for Rizell-Golovko's lifted Legendrians by counting treed disks. Our formalism of LCH allows us to obtain augmentations from certain non-exact fillings. We prove a conjecture of Rizell-Golovko relating the augmentation variety associated to the LCH of a lifted Legendrian and the disk potential of the base Lagrangian.

proof_stages.label: Chekanov-to-augmentation problem and announced answer
proof_stages.summary: The talk positions Chekanov-Eliashberg DGAs as powerful but hard-to-compute Legendrian invariants and announces that, for lifted Legendrians, augmentation varieties and ideals can be constructed and explicitly identified with a disk-potential zero set.

proof_stages.label: Prequantum and Bohr-Sommerfeld lifting setup
proof_stages.summary: The speaker defines prequantum circle bundles, Bohr-Sommerfeld Lagrangian immersions, and the covering condition that lets monotone Lagrangians lift to embedded Legendrians.

proof_stages.label: Main theorem: DGA, cobordism maps, and invariants
proof_stages.summary: Under compact integral symplectic, monotone, oriented, spin, and Bohr-Sommerfeld-cover hypotheses, the main theorem asserts a treed-disk Chekanov algebra with invariant homology, cobordism-induced DGA maps, invariant augmentation ideals/varieties, and a disk-potential formula in the connected case.

proof_stages.label: Applications via fillings and disk potentials
proof_stages.summary: The theorem is applied to obtain augmentations from non-exact fillings and to distinguish Legendrian lifts, yielding infinite families when the projected Lagrangians have inequivalent disk potentials.

proof_stages.label: Treed holomorphic disk Chekanov algebra
proof_stages.summary: The construction chooses Morse data, a compatible cylindrical almost complex structure, and capping paths, then forms a completed tensor algebra over the group ring and defines its differential by weighted counts of rigid treed holomorphic disks with Reeb-chord matching conditions.

proof_stages.label: Algebraic augmentation ideal and variety
proof_stages.summary: Augmentations of the DGA are passed to the graded realization, a Laurent polynomial ring is embedded using a basis of the free part of H_1 and Morse-dual generators, and the augmentation ideal and variety are defined by vanishing under all augmentations.

proof_stages.label: Disk-potential identification
proof_stages.summary: Using the holomorphic C^*-bundle projection from R times the prequantum bundle to the base, the differential of the smallest Reeb chord is computed as the disk-potential polynomial, giving one containment, while an algebraic construction of augmentations for every zero supplies the reverse equality.

examples.name: Clifford versus Chekanov torus disk potentials
examples.note: The explicit potentials x+y+1/(xy) and the Chekanov-torus potential are compared to demonstrate how the disk-potential formula distinguishes Legendrian lifts.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker computes the Clifford and Chekanov disk potentials, reparameterizes the resulting polynomials, and compares their ideals/varieties
